_seed_meta.description: Extract standard fields from Department of Energy awards, cooperative agreements, and lab contracts including SF-424A budget categories.

test_cases.label: BES University Grant - Perovskite Solar (UW-Madison)
test_cases.source_text:
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF ENERGY
OFFICE OF SCIENCE
FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE AWARD

Award Instrument: Grant
Award Number: DE-SC0024817

Recipient: University of Wisconsin-Madison
           21 North Park Street, Suite 6401
           Madison, WI 53715-1218
           DUNS: 161202122    EIN: 39-6006492

Funding Opportunity Announcement:
   FOA Number: DE-FOA-0003189
   Title: Basic Energy Sciences: Materials Sciences and Engineering Division
   Research Area: Solar Energy Conversion

Program Office: Office of Science (SC)
   Division: Basic Energy Sciences (BES)
   Program Manager: Dr. Helen W. Kershaw
   Phone: (301) 555-2847

---

AWARD DETAILS

Project Title: Compositional Engineering of Halide Perovskite Thin Films for
Thermally Stable High-Efficiency Photovoltaic Devices

Principal Investigator: Dr. Margaret L. Thornton
   Department of Materials Science and Engineering
   University of Wisconsin-Madison
   1509 University Avenue, Room 246
   Madison, WI 53706
   Phone: (608) 555-9374
   Email: mthornton@wisc.edu

Project Period:
   Start Date: 09/01/2025
   End Date: 08/31/2028
   Duration: 36 months

---

BUDGET SUMMARY (SF-424A)

Budget Period 1: 09/01/2025 - 08/31/2026
Budget Period 2: 09/01/2026 - 08/31/2027
Budget Period 3: 09/01/2027 - 08/31/2028

COST CATEGORY                        FEDERAL        COST SHARING     TOTAL
----------------------------------------------------------------------
a. Personnel Costs                   $285,000       $42,000          $327,000
   PI (15% effort x 3 years)          $67,500
   Postdoc (100% x 3 years)          $174,000
   Graduate Student (50% x 3 years)   $43,500

b. Fringe Benefits                    $88,350       $13,020          $101,370
   PI (31%)                            $20,925
   Postdoc (28%)                       $48,720
   Graduate (43%)                      $18,705

c. Equipment                          $0             $0               $0

d. Travel                             $18,600        $0               $18,600
   ACS National Meetings (3 trips)     $10,800
   MRS Fall Meeting (2 trips)           $7,800

e. Supplies                           $142,000       $0               $142,000
   Perovskite precursor chemicals       $48,000
   Thin film deposition targets         $35,000
   Characterization consumables         $32,000
   Glassware and lab consumables        $27,000

f. Contractual/Subawards              $0             $0               $0

g. Other Costs                        $24,600        $0               $24,600
   Publication and open access fees     $8,400
   Synchrotron beamtime user fees      $12,000
   Equipment maintenance                $4,200

h. Total Direct Costs                 $558,550       $55,020          $613,570

i. Indirect Charges                   $340,621       $49,980          $390,601
   Rate: 54.5% MTDC
   Base: $558,550 (federal) / $55,020 (cost share)

j. Total Federal Funding:             $899,171
k. Total Cost Sharing:                $105,000
l. Total Project Costs:               $1,004,171

---

NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY ACT (NEPA)

NEPA Determination: Categorical Exclusion
Categorical Exclusion Applied: B3.6 - Small-scale research and development,
process development, and small-scale pilot plant operations.
Date of Determination: 07/15/2025

---

PROJECT NARRATIVE SUMMARY

This project addresses a critical barrier to commercialization of halide perovskite
photovoltaics: thermal instability of the absorber layer at operating temperatures
above 85°C. Through systematic compositional engineering of mixed-halide
perovskite thin films (combining formamidinium, cesium, and methylammonium
cations with iodide-bromide anion mixtures), the research aims to identify
compositions that maintain >22% power conversion efficiency while surviving
1000+ hours at 85°C/85% relative humidity. Advanced characterization using
synchrotron X-ray diffraction at the Advanced Photon Source (APS) will provide
real-time insights into degradation mechanisms at the atomic scale.

expected_values.Project Title: Compositional Engineering of Halide Perovskite Thin Films for Thermally Stable High-Efficiency Photovoltaic Devices

           4800 Innovation Drive, Suite 300
           Durham, NC 27703
           DUNS: 089234567    EIN: 83-2456198

AWARD DETAILS

Project Title: Iron-Air Redox Flow Battery System for 100-Hour Grid-Scale
Energy Storage

Principal Investigator: Dr. Rajesh K. Subramanian
   Chief Technology Officer
   VoltGrid Energy Solutions, Inc.
   Phone: (919) 555-8834
   Email: rsubramanian@voltgrid.com

Project Period:
   Start Date: 03/01/2025
   End Date: 02/28/2029
   Duration: 48 months

---

BUDGET SUMMARY (SF-424A)

Phase 1 (Concept Development):   03/01/2025 - 02/28/2027 (24 months)
Phase 2 (Prototype & Test):      03/01/2027 - 02/28/2029 (24 months)

COST CATEGORY                        FEDERAL        COST SHARING     TOTAL
----------------------------------------------------------------------

b. Fringe Benefits                    $468,600       $191,400         $660,000
   Rate: 33% of personnel

c. Equipment                          $1,377,000     $423,000         $1,800,000
   Iron-air cell fabrication line       $680,000
   Electrolyte circulation system       $320,000
   Battery test stations (8 units)      $245,000
   Environmental chambers (2 units)     $132,000

d. Travel                              $86,000        $0               $86,000
   ARPA-E Annual Summit                  $18,000
   DOE review meetings (quarterly)       $48,000

e. Supplies                            $438,000       $112,000         $550,000
   Iron powder and electrode materials   $195,000
   Electrolyte chemicals                 $128,000

f. Contractual/Subawards               $1,200,000     $300,000         $1,500,000
   Georgia Institute of Technology       $750,000
   (membrane characterization and
   degradation modeling)
   Pacific Northwest Natl. Lab (PNNL)   $450,000
   (accelerated life testing)

g. Other Costs                          $168,400       $68,380          $236,780
   Patent filing and prosecution          $85,000
   Testing and certification fees         $42,000
   Insurance and facility costs           $28,400
   Publications and outreach              $13,000

h. Total Direct Costs                  $5,158,000     $1,674,780       $6,832,780

i. Indirect Charges                     $392,000       $200,000         $592,000
   Rate: 22% MTDC
   Base: Personnel + Fringe (excl. subs and equip)

j. Total Federal Funding:              $5,550,000
k. Total Cost Sharing:                 $1,874,780
l. Total Project Costs:                $7,424,780

Cost Share Percentage: 25.2% (exceeds minimum 20% requirement)

---

NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY ACT (NEPA)

NEPA Determination: Environmental Assessment
An Environmental Assessment (EA) was prepared pursuant to 10 CFR 1021.315.
The EA concluded with a Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI).
Date of FONSI: 01/22/2025
DOE/EA-2198

---

PROJECT NARRATIVE SUMMARY

VoltGrid Energy Solutions proposes to develop a novel iron-air redox flow
battery system targeting 100-hour discharge duration at $5/kWh levelized
cost of energy storage (LCOS). The technology uses earth-abundant iron as
the negative electrode active material and ambient air as the positive
electrode reactant, eliminating the need for expensive cathode materials.
Phase 1 focuses on fundamental cell chemistry optimization, achieving
>80% round-trip energy efficiency at 10 mA/cm² current density. Phase 2
scales to a 10 kW prototype system integrated with a microgrid testbed
at the National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL).

expected_values.Project Title: Iron-Air Redox Flow Battery System for 100-Hour Grid-Scale Energy Storage

U.S. DEPARTMENT OF ENERGY

Award Number: DE-EE0010342

          Low-Carbon Cement and Concrete Manufacturing
   Topic Area: Advanced Process Heating Technologies

Program Office: EERE

   Federal Project Officer: Ms. Patricia R. Gonzalez
   Phone: (720) 555-4618
   Contract Specialist: Mr. David L. Foster

AWARD DETAILS

Project Title: Microwave-Plasma Hybrid Reactor for Low-Carbon Cement Clinker
Calcination

Principal Investigator: Dr. Samuel T. Okonkwo
   Associate Professor, School of Mechanical Engineering
   Georgia Institute of Technology
   George W. Woodruff School of Mechanical Engineering
   801 Ferst Drive NW
   Atlanta, GA 30332-0405
   Phone: (404) 555-7219
   Email: sokonkwo@gatech.edu

Project Period:
   Start Date: 10/01/2025
   End Date: 09/30/2028
   Duration: 36 months

---

BUDGET SUMMARY (SF-424A)

Budget Period 1: 10/01/2025 - 09/30/2026
Budget Period 2: 10/01/2026 - 09/30/2027
Budget Period 3: 10/01/2027 - 09/30/2028

COST CATEGORY                        FEDERAL        COST SHARING     TOTAL
----------------------------------------------------------------------
a. Personnel Costs                   $412,000       $0               $412,000
   PI (20% effort x 3 years)          $78,000
   Research Scientists (2 FTE)        $216,000
   Graduate Students (2 x 50%)        $118,000

b. Fringe Benefits                    $132,640       $0               $132,640
   PI (32.2%)                          $25,116
   Research Scientists (32.2%)         $69,552
   Graduate (32.2%)                    $37,996

c. Equipment                          $385,000       $0               $385,000
   Microwave generator (100kW, 915MHz) $165,000
   Plasma torch assembly                $95,000
   High-temp kiln reactor vessel        $78,000
   Gas analysis system (FTIR+MS)        $47,000

d. Travel                              $32,400       $0               $32,400
   DOE AMO Peer Review (annual)         $10,800
   Cement industry partner visits        $12,600
   AIChE Annual Meeting (2 trips)        $9,000

e. Supplies                            $186,000       $0               $186,000
   Raw materials (limestone, clay)       $42,000
   Microwave-transparent crucibles       $38,000
   Gas handling components               $35,000
   Refractory materials                  $32,000

f. Contractual/Subawards               $975,000       $0               $975,000
   Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL) $975,000
   (neutron scattering characterization
   of clinker microstructure evolution
   and process modeling support)

g. Other Costs                          $48,460       $0               $48,460
   Publication costs                      $7,200
   Equipment maintenance contracts       $18,000
   Intellectual property costs           $14,260
   Outreach and workforce development     $9,000

h. Total Direct Costs                  $2,171,500     $0               $2,171,500

i. Indirect Charges                     $489,000       $0               $489,000
   Rate: 56.5% MTDC
   Base: $1,196,500 (excl. equipment, subs > $25K, participant support)
   Note: Subaward first $25K included in base

k. Total Cost Sharing:                 $0
   (Cost sharing not required per FOA)
l. Total Project Costs:                $2,660,500

---

NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY ACT (NEPA)

NEPA Determination: Not Applicable
Justification: All research activities are conducted within existing
laboratory facilities at Georgia Tech and ORNL. No new construction,
modification of facilities, or field activities are proposed. Research
activities fall within the scope of existing NEPA coverage for the
respective facilities.

---

PROJECT NARRATIVE SUMMARY

Cement production accounts for approximately 8% of global CO2 emissions,
with the calcination of limestone (CaCO3 -> CaO + CO2) representing 60%
of process emissions. This project develops a microwave-plasma hybrid
reactor that replaces conventional fossil-fuel-fired rotary kilns with
electrified process heating. The hybrid approach combines volumetric
microwave heating (for uniform thermal activation) with atmospheric-pressure
plasma (for rapid surface reactions), targeting a 40% reduction in energy
consumption and near-complete elimination of combustion-related CO2. The
research team will characterize clinker quality through neutron scattering
at ORNL's Spallation Neutron Source and validate scale-up models for
industrial deployment.

expected_values.Project Title: Microwave-Plasma Hybrid Reactor for Low-Carbon Cement Clinker Calcination
